item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am not sure what I just read or how this connects to SCP-2845. I did like some parts of it but I must down vote. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… feel like it's weird that this is still linked from it. Without the explicit mention that it "broke the Masquerade", it's not really clear what relation the story has to the article — the Foundation has covered up even more implausible things to cover up in other SCP entries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing with everyone above me. This is the SCP that broke the fucking Masqerade, and this tale isn't worthy to be its after math. Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So you reaaaaally wanted (for no good reason) for a(n otherwise great) SCP to break the masquerade, and we see the aftereffects in a lame (well written, still lame) tale. 'Kay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel as though I'm coming in on the tail end of something. From the list of events in SCP-2845 I gather that the arrival of it was hard if not impossible to cover up, but I must be missing some other context. Unless the point is that this SCP changed everything in terms of the Foundation?

It's very well written either way, but I still feel like there's more to this…. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's quite well-written, but I was pretty tempted to downvote on principle after reading about the awfulness of boxes, how dumb the Foundation is, etc. etc. etc. etc. etc. for the umpteen-trillionth time.

No vote for now; I'll re-read it lead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The bulk of my writing has always been tales. There will be scips, but they will, as usual, be a minority.

(And to be completely honest, this isn't actually how the masquerade is broken within my canon. This is a side thing.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, we are sort of the Foundation wiki. Tell me, do you plan to write any more SCPs again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's the same world. That's what you're writing about. Not the Foundation.

The Foundation is just a single player on a very large and lengthy stage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It raises a question of why in your boxless world, the Foundation still exists at all. If you're rejecting "S" and "C", and suggesting that "P" was never really there, what is there possibly to write about that can bear the "SCP" name?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ayup. This was added to expand upon the whole "Everything got blown up at the end of the article" thing.
